title: Multiply by 1-digit numbers with standard algorithm
description: Multiply 2, 3, and 4-digit numbers by 1-digit numbers using standard algorithm
topic_path: Multiply by 1-digit numbers with standard algorithm

assessment_items.question_content:
Yin starts to use the standard algorithm to solve $5{,}819\times8$. Her work is shown below.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{}5}{,}\overset{\blueD{y}}8\overset{7}1\overset{}9&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} 8}&\\
52& 
\end{align}$

**What number should Yin replace $\blueD{y}$ with?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, Yin multiplies $\maroonD8\times\maroonD9=\greenD{72}$. 

Yin places the $\greenD2$ ones in the ones column and carries the $\greenD7$ tens to the tens column.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{}5}{,}\overset{\blueD{}}8\overset{\greenD7}1\overset{}{\maroonD9}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD8}&\\
\greenD2& \quad \maroonD{8} \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{72} 
\end{align}$

Next, Yin multiplies ${\maroonD8\times\maroonD{1\text{ ten}}} = {8\text{ tens}}$. 


Then, she adds the $\greenD{7\text{ tens}}$.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{}5}{,}\overset{\blueD{1}}8\overset{\greenD7}{\maroonD1}\overset{}{9}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD8}&\\
\blueD5\,2&\quad (\maroonD{8} \times \maroonD{10}) + \greenD{70} = \blueD{150}\\
\end{align}$

Yin places $\blueD{5\text{ tens}}$ in the tens column and carries $\blueD{1\text{ hundred}}$ to the hundreds column.

assessment_items.question_content:
Carl starts to use the standard algorithm to solve $2{,}375\times6$. His work is shown below.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{\blueD{y}}2}{,}\overset{4}3\overset{3}7\overset{}5&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} 6}&\\
250& 
\end{align}$

**What number should Carl replace $\blueD{y}$ with?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, Carl multiplies $\maroonD6\times\maroonD5=\greenD{30}$. 

Carl places the $\greenD0$ ones in the ones column and carries the $\greenD3$ tens to the tens column.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{}2}{,}\overset{\blueD{}}3\overset{\greenD3}7\overset{}{\maroonD5}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD6}&\\
\greenD0& \quad \maroonD{6} \times \maroonD{5} = \greenD{30} 
\end{align}$

Next, Carl multiplies ${\maroonD6\times\maroonD{7\text{ tens}}} = {42\text{ tens}}$. 


Then, he adds the $\greenD{3\text{ tens}}$.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{}2}{,}\overset{\blueD{4}}3\overset{\greenD3}{\maroonD7 }\overset{}{5}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD6}&\\
\blueD5\,0&\quad (\maroonD{6} \times \maroonD{70}) + \greenD{30} = \blueD{450}\\
\end{align}$

Carl places $\blueD{5\text{ tens}}$ in the tens column and carries $\blueD{4\text{ hundreds}}$ to the hundreds column.

Then, Carl multiplies ${\maroonD6\times\maroonD{3\text{ hundreds}}} = {18\text{ hundreds}}$. 


Then, he adds the $\greenD{4\text{ hundreds}}$.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{\blueD2}2}{,}\overset{\greenD{4}}{\maroonD3}\overset{3}{7 }\overset{}{5}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD6}&\\
\blueD2\,50&\quad (\maroonD{6} \times \maroonD{300}) + \greenD{400} = \blueD{2{,}200}\\
\end{align}$

Carl places $\blueD{2\text{ hundreds}}$ in the hundreds column and carries $\blueD{2\text{ thousands}}$ to the thousands column.

assessment_items.question_content:
Don starts to use the standard algorithm to solve $418\times5$. His work is shown below.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{}4}\overset{\blueD{y}}1\overset{}8&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} 5}&\\
0& 
\end{align}$

**What number should Don replace $\blueD{y}$ with?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, Don multiplies $\maroonD5\times\maroonD8=\greenD{40}$. 

Don places the $\greenD0$ ones in the ones column and carries the $\blueD4$ tens to the tens column.

$\begin{align}
\overset{\blueD{}}4\overset{\blueD4}1\overset{}{\maroonD8}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD5}&\\
\greenD0& \quad \maroonD{5} \times \maroonD{8} = \greenD{40} 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Ann starts to use the standard algorithm to solve $592\times7$. Her work is shown below.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{\blueD{h}}5}\overset{1}9\overset{}2&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} 7}&\\
44& 
\end{align}$

**What number should Ann replace $\blueD{h}$ with?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, Ann multiplies $\maroonD2\times\maroonD7=\greenD{14}$. 

Ann places the $\greenD4$ ones in the ones column and carries the $\blueD1$ ten to the tens column.

$\begin{align}
\overset{\blueD{}}5\overset{\greenD1}9\overset{}{\maroonD2}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD7}&\\
\greenD4& \quad \maroonD{7} \times \maroonD{2} = \greenD{14} 
\end{align}$

Next, Ann multiplies ${\maroonD7\times\maroonD{9\text{ tens}}} = {63\text{ tens}}$. 


Then, she adds the $\greenD{1\text{ ten}}$.

$\begin{align}
{\overset{\blueD6}5}\overset{\greenD1}{\maroonD9}\overset{}{2}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD7}&\\
\blueD4\,4&\quad (\maroonD{7} \times \maroonD{90}) + \greenD{10} = \blueD{640}\\
\end{align}$

Ann places $\blueD{4\text{ tens}}$ in the tens column and carries $\blueD{6\text{ hundreds}}$ to the hundreds column.

assessment_items.question_content:
Boris starts to use the standard algorithm to solve $86\times9$. His work is shown below.

$\begin{align}
\overset{\blueD{y}}8\overset{}6&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} 9}&\\
4& 
\end{align}$

**What number should Boris replace $\blueD{y}$ with?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, Boris multiplies $\maroonD9\times\maroonD6=\greenD{54}$. 

Boris places the $\greenD4$ ones in the ones column and carries the $\blueD5$ tens to the tens column.

$\begin{align}
\overset{\blueD5}8\overset{}{\maroonD6}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD9}&\\
\greenD4& \quad \maroonD{9} \times \maroonD{6} = \greenD{54} 
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Lena starts to use the standard algorithm to solve $39\times4$. Her work is shown below.

$\begin{align}
\overset{\blueD{m}}3\overset{}9&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} 4}&\\
6& 
\end{align}$

**What number should Lena replace $\blueD{m}$ with?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, Lena multiplies $\maroonD4\times\maroonD9=\greenD{36}$. 

Lena places the $\greenD6$ ones in the ones column and carries the $\blueD3$ tens to the tens column.

$\begin{align}
\overset{\blueD3}3\overset{}{\maroonD9}&\\
\underline{{} \times \phantom{~~~~} \maroonD4}&\\
\greenD6& \quad \maroonD{4} \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{36} 
\end{align}$
